data.title: Smithfield Foods is the most overbought U.S. large-cap stock
data.description:
As large-cap went through a volatile month, below is a list of the top 10 large-cap stocks ranked by their Relative Strength Index, identifying the most overbought stocks across sectors including energy, packaged foods, and chemicals.

The list is topped by Smithfield Foods, Inc. (SFD), with an RSI of 79. APA Corporation (APA) and Occidental Petroleum Corporation (OXY) are next, both sharing an RSI of 76.

The energy sector dominates this list, with companies like ConocoPhillips (COP), Permian Resources Corporation (PR), and Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM) all appearing among the top stocks. While these stocks are overbought, some maintain high Quant ratings, including Exxon Mobil with a Strong Buy rating of 4.95 and LyondellBasell Industries with a Strong Buy rating of 4.80.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

Here is the list:

  1. Smithfield Foods, Inc. (SFD), RSI: 79

  2. APA Corporation (APA), RSI: 76

  3. Occidental Petroleum Corporation (OXY), RSI: 76

  4. ConocoPhillips (COP), RSI: 75

  5. Permian Resources Corporation (PR), RSI: 74

  6. Dow Inc. (DOW), RSI: 74

  7. Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM), RSI: 72

  8. LyondellBasell Industries N.V. (LYB), RSI: 72

  9. Entergy Corporation (ETR), RSI: 72

  10. United Therapeutics Corporation (UTHR), RSI: 71

data.title: Boeing supply chain shows signs of stabilization as 737 output trails target: BNP Paribas
data.description:
Production across Boeing’s (BA) commercial aircraft programs showed mixed signals in March, with supplier activity holding relatively steady even as output for the 737 program remained below target levels, according to a research note from BNP Paribas analyst Matthew Akers.

737 fuselage shipments remain below goal

Akers said tracking of fuselage shipments from Wichita, Kansas to Renton, Washington, suggests production in the high-30s per month range. Approximately 38 fuselages were observed moving by rail in March, modestly below Boeing’s (BA) stated goal of producing 42 aircraft per month.

The shipments, which originate from the former Spirit AeroSystems facility in Wichita, are used as a proxy for production levels, though Akers noted that monthly figures may not align precisely with Boeing’s (BA) internal production cadence.

787 production appears near plan

Separately, activity from Boeing’s (BA) Dreamlifter transport aircraft indicates that output of the 787 program is close to expectations. Around 94 Dreamlifter flights were tracked in March, implying production near the company’s target rate of roughly eight aircraft a month.

The modified cargo planes are used to move large aircraft components between global suppliers and final assembly sites, making flight activity a key indicator of production trends.

Suppliers seen benefiting as destocking eases

Akers said aerospace original equipment suppliers appear to be recovering as inventory reductions across the supply chain begin to taper off. He argued that current market expectations may underestimate growth prospects for suppliers tied to aircraft production.

Among those, BNP Paribas maintains positive ratings on Howmet Aerospace (HWM) and RTX (RTX), while holding a more cautious view on Boeing (BA), citing potential downside risks to cash flow expectations.

The analysis comes as Boeing (BA) continues to navigate production constraints and quality-related issues, including a recent wiring concern affecting its 737 Max program, while working to stabilize output across its commercial aircraft lines.

data.title: Gilead extends Arcellx tender offer expiration date
data.description:
• Gilead Sciences (GILD) has extended the expiration date of its tender offer for all common shares of Arcellx (ACLX) to 5p ET on April 24. It was midnight on April 3 prior.
  • The biotech giant noted  that as of 5p ET on March 31, only ~7.5% of outstanding shares have been tendered.
  • The deal, worth up to $7.8B, has already been approved by both companies' boards of directors and is expected to close in Q2. 
  • Terms call for Gilead to pay $115 per Arcellx share in cash at closing and one contingent value right of $5 per share.

data.title: White Mountains Partners to acquire BaseSix
data.description:
• White Mountains Partners (WMP), a White Mountains (WTM) operating company, has acquired a majority interest in BaseSix Systems.
  • Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Marietta, Georgia, BaseSix provides the design, installation, retrofit, maintenance, and repair of mission-critical, low-voltage building systems for commercial and institutional customers across the fire & life safety, network & wireless, security & access control, and audio-visual disciplines. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple maintains shine at 50 as it cautiously enters AI era
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) officially turned 50 today, and in that time frame, no other company has transformed humankind's relationship with technology more than the one created by college dropouts in a garage.

The Apple I was little more than a circuit board. It didn't include a monitor, keyboard, or casing. Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak had a vision to create a user-friendly computer that was small and affordable enough for people to place in their homes and offices. In the summer of 1977, they introduced the Apple II. It was one of the first mass-produced computers featuring color graphics and an integrated keyboard. The company generated $7.8M in sales revenue in 1978. That jumped to $117M by 1980.

iPod Shuffle to the present day, and Apple still hasn't lost its shine. During fiscal year 2025, Apple reported record-setting revenue of $416B. That's more than the GDP of 176 countries, according to IMF data. Apple enjoys a market capitalization of $3.75T, second in the world only to Nvidia (NVDA). About 27% of the world's population, or 2.2B people, use its products, giving it the largest installed base of any single brand.

Apple took its fiftieth anniversary seriously, holding a series of events and concerts around the globe leading up to April 1. Sir Paul McCartney performed at Apple Park in Cupertino, Calif., on Tuesday night.

"From the first Apple computer to the Mac, from iPod to iPhone, iPad to Apple Watch and AirPods, as well as the services we use every day — the App Store, Apple Music, Apple Pay, iCloud, and Apple TV — we've spent five decades rethinking what's possible and putting powerful tools into people's hands," said CEO Tim Cook. "Through every breakthrough, one idea has guided us — that the world is moved forward by people who think different."  

Although Apple has come under recent scrutiny for not taking the lead in the artificial intelligence race, its patient approach is likely to pay off in the long run. Apple's intense focus on user privacy is nearly anathema to the large language models that gather vast amounts of personal information. As other Mag 7 companies are dumping unprecedented amounts of capex into AI, Apple is demonstrating restraint.

"Apple has a long history of executing on the second-entrant advantage, and I look forward to seeing whether it can do the same in AI, as I suspect that its vast ecosystem may prove more important than having the very best model," said Julian Lin, Investing Group Leader for Best Of Breed Growth Stocks.

Apple has proven it can weather any type of storm. Despite a smartphone market flooded with competition and memory headwinds, its iPhone revenue jumped 23% during the first quarter of fiscal 2026. PCs and laptops are facing similar issues, yet the release of the MacBook Neo last month produced its best week ever in attracting first-time Mac buyers. Throw in geopolitical chaos featuring trade wars, tariffs, and international conflict, and Apple says, "Hold my boba tea." 

"At 50, Apple is transitioning from a hardware-driven giant into an ecosystem powered by AI, services, and tightly integrated user experiences," said Seeking Alpha analyst Yiannis Zourmpanos. "The real upside lies in monetizing its massive installed base through higher-margin services and embedding intelligence across devices. What comes next isn't a single breakthrough product but a compounding platform that gets smarter, stickier, and more indispensable over time."

"At Apple, we're more focused on building tomorrow than remembering yesterday," Cook said in a blog post. "But we couldn't let this milestone pass without thanking the millions of people who make Apple what it is today — our incredible teams around the world, our developer community, and every customer who has joined us on this journey."

data.title: Leapmotor is the latest Chinese automaker to make moves in the Americas
data.description:
Stellantis NV (STLA) is in early talks about building electric vehicles in Canada with Chinese partner Zhejiang Leapmotor Technology, sources told Bloomberg. 

Earlier this year, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney reached an agreement with President Xi Jinping in January to reduce tariffs on Chinese-made EVs and said the government was open to new Chinese joint-venture investments with trusted partners in the Canadian auto sector.

While Trump Administration officials warned in the past of retaliation if Canada uses a back door to export Chinese vehicles into the U.S., the talks have still moved forward. Chinese electric vehicle makers have already set up operations in Central America and South America. 

Stellantis' (STLA) partnership with Zhejiang Leapmotor Technology centers on a global electric-vehicle alliance in which Stellantis (STLA) leverages Leapmotor’s low-cost EV platforms and technology while giving Leapmotor access to Stellantis' (STLA) distribution and manufacturing footprint outside China. As part of the arrangement, Stellantis (STLA) took an approximately 20% equity stake in Leapmotor for about €1.5B.

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Thursday
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes moved higher on Wednesday, building on recent momentum as optimism grew around a potential easing of the Iran conflict.

Leading the advance was the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) as it added +1.1%, while the S&P 500 (SP500) gained +0.7% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) rose +0.4%.

Now, here are three focus points for investors on Thursday: 

  • Market participants will be focused on how markets digest Donald Trump’s 9:00 p.m. EST address to the nation, as the ongoing Middle East conflict continues to rattle investor sentiment. The speech will be closely analyzed for signals on potential policy direction, geopolitical de-escalation or escalation talks, and any implications for energy markets and broader risk appetite.
  • Traders will be closely watching Thursday’s release of initial jobless claims data before the open, as investors look for fresh insight into labor market conditions and the overall resilience of the U.S. economy. The report will be closely analyzed for signs of easing or persistent tightness in employment trends, which could shape expectations around consumer strength and the Federal Reserve’s policy path.
  • Investors will also be eyeing the release of balance of trade data before the bell, as investors look for fresh insight into global demand flows and the strength of the U.S. external position. The report will be closely analyzed for shifts in exports and imports, which could influence GDP expectations and provide clues on currency and manufacturing trends.

data.title: IGV Software ETF crashes 24% in worst quarter since '08, Quant rates top holdings
data.description:
The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF ($IGV) cratered more than 24% in Q1 2026, its worst quarterly plunge since Q4 2008, erasing gains and dragging software names into bear territory amid AI disruption fears and growth slowdowns, FactSet data shows.

Below is a list of the top 10 holdings of the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. The list highlights stocks within the technology and software sectors based on their quantitative performance metrics.

Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) leads the list with the highest Quant Rating of 3.47 among the group. Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and Oracle Corporation (ORCL) follow closely behind with ratings of 3.42 and 3.39, respectively.

All top holdings currently carry “Hold” ratings according to the Quant system, with scores ranging from 3.47 at the top down to 2.79 at the bottom of the list. This indicates a neutral outlook across the leading software sector holdings.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades stocks based on their relative performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. Microsoft Corporation (MSFT), Quant Rating: 3.47

  2. Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR), Quant Rating: 3.42

  3. Oracle Corporation (ORCL), Quant Rating: 3.39

  4. AppLovin Corporation (APP), Quant Rating: 3.26

  5. ServiceNow, Inc. (NOW), Quant Rating: 3.19

  6. Salesforce, Inc. (CRM), Quant Rating: 3.12

  7. CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. (CRWD), Quant Rating: 3.06

  8. Adobe Inc. (ADBE), Quant Rating: 3.06

  9. Intuit Inc. (INTU), Quant Rating: 2.80

  10. Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW), Quant Rating: 2.79

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Chevron confirms exclusive power supply deal with Microsoft, Engine No. 1
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) confirmed Thursday it entered into an exclusivity agreement with Microsoft (MSFT) and investment fund Engine No. 1 for the negotiation of a proposed power generation and electricity offtake arrangement, although no commercial terms have been finalized, and no definitive agreement has been reached so far.

Bloomberg had reported Microsoft's (MSFT) involvement in a proposed natural gas-fired power plant in West Texas with a projected cost of ~$7B that would initially generate 2,500 MW of electricity to power ​a large ⁠data center campus.

Microsoft (MSFT) is aggressively building data centers as it battles for supremacy in artificial intelligence, increasing its AI capacity by 80% last year and planning to double its data center footprint over the next two years, and access to reliable baseload power is a key challenge—one that the Chevron (CVX) and Engine No. 1 partnership expects to address given its extensive natural gas production in West Texas and contracts for large turbines.

"While we are in exclusive negotiations, and subject to the execution of definitive agreements and certain conditions being satisfactorily met, the parties do not intend to comment further," Chevron (CVX) said in a statement.

data.title: XLRE holds up better than S&P 500; EQIX, IRM, DLR lead gains
data.description:
The Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) posted a slight increase of 1.11% in FY26 Q1, while the broader S&P 500 Index fell ~4.6%, which shows REITs performed relatively better than the overall equities ongoing crash.

However, the housing market is still weak. Existing home sales are still ~25% lower than 2019 levels and are at their weakest since 1995. Small drops in mortgage rates below 6% led to short-term refinancing activity, but rates have climbed again due to geopolitical tensions. 

This weak housing trend could drag the U.S. economy down and hurt consumer sentiment as 2026 moves forward, said Bret Jensen, a market analyst and investing group leader at Seeking Alpha.

Mortgage rates reportedly increased last week. The 30-year rate rose to 6.38% from 6.22%, but it is still lower than 6.65% a year ago. Similarly, the 15-year rate also rose to 5.75% from 5.54%, slightly lower than last year.

On the positive side, according to Sam Khater of Freddie Mac, the housing market is slowly improving. More people are applying for home loans compared to last year, and rates are still better on an annual basis.

Amid the ongoing market pressure, investors are again focusing on premium real estate assets, as per Jonathan Goldstein, co-founder and CEO of Cain International. He remained positive about long-term opportunities in the Middle East, expecting strong growth in the Gulf region over the next 10-15 years.

Top 5 Performers (Q1 2026):

  • Equinix (EQIX) +25.83%
  • Iron Mountain (IRM) +17.66%
  • Digital Realty Trust (DLR) +13.23%
  • Kimco Realty (KIM) +10.11%
  • Regency Centers (REG) +8.49%

Bottom 5 Performers (Q1 2026):

  • CoStar Group (CSGP) -39.20%
  • Boston Properties (BXP) -23.21%
  • CBRE Group (CBRE) -17.20%
  • Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) -12.37%
  • SBA Communications (SBAC) -12.20%
